title: centaurea jurineifolia
intro: Centaurea jurineifolia, also known as Jurine's knapweed, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family native to France and Italy. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. Centaurea jurineifolia can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various ailments.
